categories.name: أَفْعَالُ اللِّسَانِ فِي الْعِبَادَةِ
categories.nameEn: Acts of the Tongue in Worship

items.verse: تُسَبِّحُ لَهُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ ٱلسَّبْعُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ وَمَن فِيهِنَّ ۚ وَإِن مِّن شَىْءٍ إِلَّا يُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِهِۦ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا تَفْقَهُونَ تَسْبِيحَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ حَلِيمًا غَفُورًۭا
items.verseEn: The seven heavens and the earth and whatever is in them exalt Him. And there is not a thing except that it exalts [Allāh] by His praise, but you do not understand their [way of] exalting. Indeed, He is ever Forbearing and Forgiving.
[All] praise is [due] to Allāh, Lord of the worlds -

items.verse: أَفَلَا يَتُوبُونَ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَيَسْتَغْفِرُونَهُۥ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: So will they not repent to Allāh and seek His forgiveness? And Allāh is Forgiving and Merciful.

items.verse: إِن تَكْفُرُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ عَنكُمْ ۖ وَلَا يَرْضَىٰ لِعِبَادِهِ ٱلْكُفْرَ ۖ وَإِن تَشْكُرُوا۟ يَرْضَهُ لَكُمْ ۗ وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌۭ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ ۗ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُم مَّرْجِعُكُمْ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ
items.verseEn: If you disbelieve - indeed, Allāh is Free from need of you. And He does not approve for His servants disbelief. And if you are grateful, He approves [i.e., likes] it for you; and no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you about what you used to do. Indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts.

categories.name: أَحْدَاثُ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ فِي سُورَةِ الِانْفِطَارِ
categories.nameEn: Events of the Day of Judgment in Surah Al-Infitar

items.verseEn: And when the [contents of] graves are scattered [i.e., exposed],
A soul will [then] know what it has put forth and kept back.

items.verse: فِىٓ أَىِّ صُورَةٍۢ مَّا شَآءَ رَكَّبَكَ
items.verseEn: In whatever form He willed has He assembled you.
No! But you deny the Recompense.

categories.name: الصِّفَاتُ الإِلٰهِيَّةُ فِي خَاتِمَةِ الْحَشْرِ
categories.nameEn: Divine Attributes in the Conclusion of Al-Hashr

items.verse: فَتَعَـٰلَى ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۖ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ رَبُّ ٱلْعَرْشِ ٱلْكَرِيمِ
items.verseEn: So exalted is Allāh, the Sovereign, the Truth; there is no deity except Him, Lord of the Noble Throne.

items.verse: وَٱلسَّلَـٰمُ عَلَىَّ يَوْمَ وُلِدتُّ وَيَوْمَ أَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ أُبْعَثُ حَيًّۭا
items.verseEn: And peace is on me the day I was born and the day I will die and the day I am raised alive."

items.verse: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى يَبْدَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥ وَهُوَ أَهْوَنُ عَلَيْهِ ۚ وَلَهُ ٱلْمَثَلُ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ
items.verseEn: And it is He who begins creation; then He repeats it, and that is [even] easier for Him. To Him belongs the highest description [i.e., attribute] in the heavens and earth. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

items.verse: وَلَهُ ٱلْكِبْرِيَآءُ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ
items.verseEn: And to Him belongs [all] grandeur within the heavens and the earth, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

categories.name: عَنَاصِرُ قِصَّةِ أَصْحَابِ الْكَهْفِ
categories.nameEn: Elements from the Story of the Companions of the Cave

items.verse: وَرَبَطْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ إِذْ قَامُوا۟ فَقَالُوا۟ رَبُّنَا رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ لَن نَّدْعُوَا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ إِلَـٰهًۭا ۖ لَّقَدْ قُلْنَآ إِذًۭا شَطَطًا
items.verseEn: And We bound [i.e., made firm] their hearts when they stood up and said, "Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. Never will we invoke besides Him any deity. We would have certainly spoken, then, an excessive transgression.

items.verse: هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ قَوْمُنَا ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ ءَالِهَةًۭ ۖ لَّوْلَا يَأْتُونَ عَلَيْهِم بِسُلْطَـٰنٍۭ بَيِّنٍۢ ۖ فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًۭا
items.verseEn: These, our people, have taken besides Him deities. Why do they not bring for [worship of] them a clear evidence? And who is more unjust than one who invents about Allāh a lie?"

items.verse: سَيَقُولُونَ ثَلَـٰثَةٌۭ رَّابِعُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ وَيَقُولُونَ خَمْسَةٌۭ سَادِسُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ رَجْمًۢا بِٱلْغَيْبِ ۖ وَيَقُولُونَ سَبْعَةٌۭ وَثَامِنُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ ۚ قُل رَّبِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ بِعِدَّتِهِم مَّا يَعْلَمُهُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌۭ ۗ فَلَا تُمَارِ فِيهِمْ إِلَّا مِرَآءًۭ ظَـٰهِرًۭا وَلَا تَسْتَفْتِ فِيهِم مِّنْهُمْ أَحَدًۭا
items.verseEn: They [i.e., people] will say there were three, the fourth of them being their dog; and they will say there were five, the sixth of them being their dog - guessing at the unseen; and they will say there were seven, and the eighth of them was their dog. Say, [O Muḥammad], "My Lord is most knowing of their number. None knows them except a few. So do not argue about them except with an obvious argument and do not inquire about them among [the speculators] from anyone."

items.verse: قُلِ ٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا لَبِثُوا۟ ۖ لَهُۥ غَيْبُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ أَبْصِرْ بِهِۦ وَأَسْمِعْ ۚ مَا لَهُم مِّن دُونِهِۦ مِن وَلِىٍّۢ وَلَا يُشْرِكُ فِى حُكْمِهِۦٓ أَحَدًۭا
items.verseEn: Say, "Allāh is most knowing of how long they remained. He has [knowledge of] the unseen [aspects] of the heavens and the earth. How Seeing is He and how Hearing! They have not besides Him any protector, and He shares not His legislation with anyone."

harf.hint: A number that signifies the oneness of God.

deduction.title: The travelers of the verdant field
deduction.intro: In an ancient time, two individuals tended neighboring lands. Their paths were intertwined, yet their hearts led them toward vastly different destinations. One walked the straight path, while the other strayed into the darkness of denial. Can you unravel the mystery of these two keepers of the harvest?
clues: I possessed abundant crops, luxuriant harvests that dazzled the eyes of onlookers.
My neighbor possessed even greater bounty than I did, yet I coveted their portion more than my own.
I suggested we destroy their crops entirely, to consolidate everything under my control.
I brought ruin upon my own harvest as well, destroying everything in my foolish scheming.
I stood helpless as my crops withered and my wealth turned to dust in a single night.
I had no share in the Hereafter, and my final abode became a pit of blazing fire.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The man who denied resurrection and judgment
The ruler who oppressed the righteous farmer
The wealthy landowner who lacked gratitude
The farmer who coveted their neighbor's land
The treacherous partner who plotted destruction
identity.answer: The farmer who coveted their neighbor's land

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: The test of receiving divine revelation
The challenge of wealth and patience
The temptation to destroy a righteous person's livelihood
The command to divide inheritance fairly
The struggle against a tyrant's oppression
trial.answer: The temptation to destroy a righteous person's livelihood

location.label: Key Element
options: The verdant gardens of paradise
Two neighboring fields of crops
The mountains that witnessed destruction
The sacred sanctuary of Makkah
The riverbanks of a blessed land

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Eternal paradise as a reward for righteousness
Complete destruction of both harvests in this life
Exile and wandering until death
Temporary prosperity followed by eternal fire
A miraculous restoration and doubled bounty
outcome.answer: Complete destruction of both harvests in this life

deduction.verse: Then they approached one another, blaming each other. (68:30)

translations: O you who believe O you who believe
scramble.hint: A beloved call to abundant remembrance of Allah — a foundational verse about dhikr.
scramble.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ذِكْرًۭا كَثِيرًۭا
scramble.verseEn: O you who have believed, remember Allāh with much remembrance
